mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates applied to equity cash flows, which compresses valuation multiples and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform baseline by more than 2 percent annualized over a 5-year out-of-sample backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns show no statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 during early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds the 220 index threshold, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances above the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of increasing total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending expectations and supports positive quarterly equity performance.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when tested out-of-sample across varying economic conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic growth that translates into higher corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles and market environments.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production below the 90.0 index level signals severe supply-side disruption and manufacturing weakness, prompting rapid equity liquidation over the following week.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of triggered observations during contractionary phases.
